intro: The Guajava anceps, also known as Psidium guajava, is a species of flowering plant in the myrtle family, commonly referred to as the guava tree. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as wound healing, anti-inflammatory effects, and antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable resource for treating various ailments. The bioactive constituents of Guajava anceps include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. Guajava anceps is commonly used to make various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat digestive issues, skin problems, and respiratory infections.
